rationale: The cited 2001 paper appears directly relevant to the citing study’s core constructs: it defines phonetic/semantic/phonosemantic translation methods in the Chinese market, while the 2019 paper’s four-way typology uses these same name-type dimensions (plus alphanumeric) to model demand. The citing note explicitly frames its contribution as extending prior lab-based brand-naming work to field sales outcomes, which makes this citation plausibly foundational rather than incidental; the DOI match confirms the specific reference.
